title: Reset the Tipper After an E-Stop From the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the documented HMI recovery sequence on the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) after an emergency stop: press ESTOP RESET, then AUTO, then CYCLE START to restart the system.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the tipper system has experienced an emergency stop and the operator is restoring operation from the Visu_MCP_Dual operator station HMI using the documented ESTOP RESET sequence.
not_for: Do not use this procedure for non-E-stop faults that require RESET or AUTO REF functions.
Do not use this procedure when additional recovery beyond ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START is required, because the source does not provide further detail in this section.

responsibilities: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual screen at the operator station.
Identify the ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START controls.
Execute the documented restart sequence after an E-stop.

safety_notes: The source states that ESTOP RESET resets the system after an E-stop.
Use only the documented HMI sequence provided by the source: ESTOP RESET, then AUTO, then CYCLE START.
No additional E-stop safety checks or field reset actions are provided in the source section.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Operator access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The tipper is being recovered after an emergency stop.
The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is available for use.

external_references.quote_or_summary: The ESTOP RESET resets the system after an E-stop. Press AUTO, then CYCLE START to restart the system.

visual_references.description: Primary operator HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel showing the main control buttons on the Operator Station Control Panel, including AUTO and cycle controls.
System screen artifact with nearby startup and reset context including clearing a flashing ESTOP and operator station cycle controls.
Operator station HMI screen artifact showing related Visu_Dual_MCP controls used in maintenance procedures.
Operator station HMI artifact associated with Visu_Dual_MCP screen usage and restart-related controls.
Photo showing operator station controls used to return the robot to automatic operation, including AUTO and CYCLE START in a related recovery context.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and identify the reset and restart controls
steps.purpose: Ensure the operator is on the correct HMI screen and can locate the controls used in the documented post-E-stop recovery sequence.
steps.instruction: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen and identify the ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START buttons on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The operator is on the primary tipper control screen and has identified the controls needed for the recovery sequence.
steps.healthy_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is visible and the operator can locate the ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START controls. Active buttons may be indicated by green illuminated bars above the buttons.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen or cannot identify the required controls.

actions.label: Open main tipper control screen
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen.

actions.label: Identify recovery controls
actions.exact_action: Identify the ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Main control buttons on the Operator Station Control Panel for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, including AUTO and cycle controls; use this figure to orient to the primary HMI layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is not available at the operator station.
The required controls cannot be identified on the HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) has buttons that control tipper movement and allows for fault reset. The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active. NOTE: This is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.
Figure 4-19 appears on page 47 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_2_1_tipper_machine_control_panel_visu_mcp_dual_screen.

steps.title: Press ESTOP RESET
steps.purpose: Reset the system after the emergency stop using the documented HMI control.
steps.instruction: Press ESTOP RESET to reset the system after an E-stop.
steps.expected_result: The system is reset from the E-stop condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts the ESTOP RESET input and is ready for the next restart actions.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not reset after ESTOP RESET.

actions.label: Reset after E-stop
actions.exact_action: Press ESTOP RESET to reset the system after an E-stop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station control panel figure to locate the reset-related controls on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not reset after pressing ESTOP RESET.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The ESTOP RESET resets the system after an E-stop.

steps.title: Press AUTO
steps.purpose: Place the tipper into automatic mode as part of the documented restart sequence.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is placed in automatic mode.
steps.healthy_condition: The system is in Auto mode and ready for cycle start.
steps.failure_condition: AUTO does not place the system into automatic mode or the system is not ready for cycle start.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the AUTO button on the primary operator station control panel.
Related operator station controls showing AUTO used to return to automatic operation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not enter automatic mode.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode.
Press AUTO, then CYCLE START to restart the system.

steps.title: Press CYCLE START
steps.purpose: Restart the tipper operation cycle after the system has been reset and placed in Auto mode.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE START to restart the system.
steps.expected_result: The system restarts from the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper operation cycle starts while the system is in Auto mode.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not restart after CYCLE START.

actions.label: Start cycle
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE START to restart the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the CYCLE START control on the primary operator station control panel.
Related operator station controls showing CYCLE START used to return operation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not restart after pressing CYCLE START.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode.
Press AUTO, then CYCLE START to restart the system.

success_criteria: The system is reset after the E-stop.
The tipper is placed in Auto mode.
The system restarts from the operator station HMI after CYCLE START.
healthy_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is accessible and the required controls are identifiable.
ESTOP RESET resets the system after the E-stop.
AUTO places the tipper in automatic mode.
CYCLE START restarts the tipper operation cycle.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the required controls cannot be identified.
The system does not reset after ESTOP RESET.
The system does not enter Auto mode after pressing AUTO.
The system does not restart after pressing CYCLE START.
escalation_guidance: If the system does not restart after ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START, escalate because the source section does not provide additional recovery detail.
If the operator cannot access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen or identify the required controls, stop and seek additional support.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary Visu_MCP_Dual operator station control panel and the main control buttons used in this procedure.
Related startup/reset context including ESTOP clearing and operator station cycle controls.
Related operator station HMI layout for Visu_Dual_MCP controls.
Related operator station HMI screen associated with restart-related controls.
Related operator station controls showing AUTO and CYCLE START in a recovery context.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The ESTOP RESET resets the system after an E-stop. Press AUTO, then CYCLE START to restart the system.

missing_details: The source does not provide additional troubleshooting or alternate recovery steps if the system does not restart after ESTOP RESET, AUTO, and CYCLE START.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this procedure.
The source does not specify LOTO requirements for this HMI recovery sequence.
The source section does not provide explicit confirmation indicators for successful ESTOP RESET beyond restart behavior.
